actionable_feedback: Add a plain-text export (CSV or .txt) that lists the workbook's key formula cells and their formulas for each row (error calculation, adjusted values for each remediation option, and scenario cost sums).

Ensure the .xlsx stores visible formulas in the cells (not only precomputed values embedded in zipped OOXML content) so programmatic tools (openpyxl) can parse them. If environment limitations persist, include a separate file with the exact Excel formulas used.

Untruncate the expert_memo.md: include a compact table showing for every record (R1,R2,R3) the raw inputs, abs diff numerator, denominator, absolute percent error (numerator/denominator), pass/fail for the >15% rule, porosity support flag, and the results after each remediation option (adjusted values, recalculated percent errors, and scenario cost).
Explicitly annotate in the memo which artifact (worksheet name and cell ranges) contains each calculation so a human verifier can quickly cross-check the spreadsheet.
Add a short note in the memo explaining that the model-based semantic grading used the memo + trace because programmatic parsing failed, and cite the file(s) you added to enable automated checks.
artifact_specific_comments: CFD_surrogate_4.xlsx: Created and present, but environment could not parse it programmatically (openpyxl_unavailable). Workbook metadata in trace indicates formula_blocks were added; include a text export of those formula blocks for automated verification.

expert_memo.md: Contains correct arithmetic and reasoning but the artifact preview was truncated in the pipeline. Ensure the full memo is included in the revision and that it contains a compact verification table.
feedback_summary: Deliverables present and math in the expert memo is consistent with the input packet; deterministic artifact-contract and model grading passed. Primary weaknesses are machine-verifiability gaps: the .xlsx could not be parsed programmatically in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview delivered to the grader was truncated. These limitations mean automated verifiers relied on the memo preview and trace rather than direct, machine-validated workbook formulas. Recommended fixes are targeted and low-effort: include a full text export of key spreadsheet formula cells and a concise per-row before/after calculation table in the memo, and ensure the workbook includes visible formulas or a worksheet with plain-text formula cells so verifiers can validate automatically.
